summary: Large item-system decomp cleanup whose stated goal was to bring all item translation units above 95% matching. The PR touched 22 files, converted four item object files from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, improved or completed many item functions/data blocks, and adjusted item headers/symbol visibility to support matching. The decomp.dev report recorded +7840 matched-code bytes, +9756 linked-code bytes, 26 new matches, and 22 additional improvements in unmatched items.

key_files.why: Marked itlgunbeam.c, itgamewatchjudge.c, itoldkuri.c, and itgreatfoxlaser.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Changed many item-related generated/address symbols from global names to local @NNN labels and local scope, including strings, floats, doubles, and itLgunbeam motion callbacks.
Largest code cleanup by deleted lines; rewrote item-spawn/random-selection logic, improved bit-mask iteration, used clearer locals, and added a dont_inline pragma around it_8026CA4C.
Corrected Mewtwo Shadow Ball item variable and dat-attribute field types/layout, replacing an opaque float array with named scalar fields.
Major matching work with several functions reaching 100% according to the bot report, including it_802BE65C and it_802BF28C.
Added substantial code; bot reported large improvements for it_802A7384 and it_802A6474, with it_802A7384 rising to 97.80%.
Completed multiple lgunbeam functions and became a Matching object.
Completed code/data portions and became a Matching object.
Added missing Old Kuri matching work, including data and it_2725_Logic0_DmgReceived, and became a Matching object.
Improved several shadow ball functions and relied on corrected item-vars/dat-attrs struct layout.

what_changed: Raised item translation-unit matching coverage toward the PR title target of all item TUs above 95%.
Converted four item source files in configure.py from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...): itlgunbeam.c, itgamewatchjudge.c, itoldkuri.c, and itgreatfoxlaser.c.
Bot report showed 26 new matches, including data or functions in itoldkuri, itnessyoyo, itgamewatchjudge, itgreatfoxlaser, itlgunbeam, it_2E5A, itlinkboomerang, it_266F, and itmewtwoshadowball.

Bot report showed 22 additional improvements in still-unmatched items, notably large gains in itlinkhookshot and multiple Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Ness Yo-Yo, item spawn, Link Bomb, Bombhei, and Seak Chain functions.
Updated GALE01 symbols so many item literals/constants are local @NNN symbols instead of global it_80xxxxxx symbols; examples include strings in .data/.sdata and floats/doubles in .sdata2.
Changed itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_Anim, itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_Coll symbol scopes from global to local while retaining their function names.
Corrected itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars field types: x0 became f32, x24 became u32, and x48 became s32.
Expanded itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs from a trailing f32 x1C[9] array into individually typed fields x1C, x20, x24, x28, x2C, x30, x34, x38, and x3C, with x20 as s32.
Reworked it_266F.c item-spawn/random-selection routines to use clearer mask, index, pointer, count, range, stage_mask, stage_info, and weight locals rather than raw var_rNN temporaries.

Removed an M2C_ERROR alternative block from it_8026CA4C and replaced it with a clean implementation using a u64 mask, pointer iteration, floating-point weighted accumulation, and assignment to alloc->x8.
Changed it_8026CA4C signature from a u16* first argument to HSD_ObjAllocUnk6* alloc and protected it with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop.
smart_moves: The PR did not only toggle configure.py to Matching; it also adjusted symbols, headers, and source expression shapes needed to make the matching status credible.
Localizing anonymous constants and strings in symbols.txt reduced unnecessary global symbol exposure and matched how compiler-local literals are represented by @NNN labels.
Correcting item struct field types was a high-leverage move: f32 versus s32/u32 fields directly affect loads, stores, casts, and floating-point codegen in item behavior functions.
Replacing broad array typing in itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs with per-offset scalar fields preserved layout while allowing individual fields to have the type needed by observed codegen.
Using a dedicated no-inline pragma around it_8026CA4C suggests the author isolated a function whose call boundary mattered for matching, instead of perturbing surrounding code.

The it_266F.c rewrites retained low-level matching-sensitive details such as explicit 64-bit mask shifting, f32 accumulation casts, and staged pointer/index increments while making the code more readable.
Scoping temporary variables inside small blocks, such as range/random-timer calculation, likely helped register allocation/lifetime without falling back to unreadable M2C output.
Converting only the four item objects that had enough evidence to be Matching kept the build configuration aligned with actual measured progress.
decomp_lessons: For item TU matching, check source, headers, configure.py, and symbols.txt together; a function may fail due to wrong struct field type or symbol scope rather than only local C syntax.
When anonymous constants are compiler-local, prefer local @NNN symbols with scope:local over exporting address-based global names.
Do not leave M2C_ERROR fallback blocks in final matching code when a clean low-level implementation can express the same register/mask behavior.
64-bit item masks may need explicit helper-style shifting patterns such as __shr2u or carefully shaped mask >>= 1 loops, depending on the target codegen.
Weighted item-spawn calculations are sensitive to f32 casts and integer accumulation order; keep the observed `(f32) sum + ((weight * (f32) *p) + 0.99f)` style when matching similar code.
Object-file matching promotion should be backed by decomp.dev or equivalent before/after evidence, especially for item files with data and sdata2 changes.
Header field-type corrections can unlock many function improvements at once, especially for item-specific ItemVars and DatAttrs overlays.
When a TU is near completion, remaining bytes may come from data sections, local string/float labels, and function scope rather than large C rewrites.
naming_conventions: Anonymous local data symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt use @NNN labels with scope:local, e.g. @181, @182, @211, @277.
Address-derived item symbols remain in the it_80xxxxxx style when still global or not yet semantically named, e.g. it_803F7918 and it_804D5708.
Item motion callback names preserve the item/module prefix and motion role, e.g. itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_Anim, itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_Phys, itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_Coll.
Unknown item struct fields continue to use offset-like xNN names, but the PR improves their scalar types rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
Cleaner local variable names in matching C describe roles such as bit_idx, word, alloc, mask, p, sum, stage_mask, stage_info, weight, and range instead of raw var_rNN temporaries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop around it_8026CA4C to preserve a needed function boundary/code shape.
Replaced a u16* aliasing first parameter with HSD_ObjAllocUnk6* alloc in it_8026CA4C, then wrote the result through alloc->x8, matching the apparent structure access.
Maintained explicit floating-point conversion order in weighted sums: cast current integer sum to f32, multiply weight by casted table entry, add 0.99f, then assign back to s32.
Used 64-bit mask iteration for item selection and stage filtering, including shifts and low-word tracking in it_8026CD50.
Adjusted variable lifetimes and register pressure by introducing scoped temporaries for random range/timer calculations.
Removed unnecessary casts on HSD_MemAlloc assignments where the destination field type already carried the needed type.
Corrected symbol scope for local floats/doubles/strings in sdata/sdata2/data so data matching aligned with compiler-generated local constants.
Used pointer-and-index loops over item-kind ranges such as It_Kind_Capsule to It_Kind_L_Gun_Ray rather than high-level constructs that might perturb codegen.

review_feedback.feedback: Author described the goal humorously as a stepping stone: after all item TUs >95%, future targets would be 97.5%, 98.75%, 99.375%, 99.6875%, etc.
GALE01 matched code increased to 65.25%, +0.20% and +7840 bytes.
Linked code increased to 37.33%, +0.25% and +9756 bytes.
Matched data increased to 38.60%, +0.03% and +376 bytes; linked data increased to 32.89%, +0.04% and +480 bytes.
Reported 26 new matches and 22 improvements in unmatched items.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that pursue the PR body's suggested next thresholds: item TUs >97.5%, >98.75%, or similar.
Search for subsequent work on itlinkhookshot.c functions it_802A7384 and it_802A6474, since they improved greatly but were not both 100% here.
Search for later Mewtwo Shadow Ball struct renames or semantic field names replacing xNN fields in itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars and itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs.
Audit other item symbols for global it_80xxxxxx constants that should be local @NNN labels.
Look for PRs that remove remaining NonMatching item objects after this threshold push.
